topic: CLI-fallback feasibility for mk_skill_advisor: skill-advisor-specific deltas against the settled spec-memory CLI-over-daemon pattern

focus: KQ1 parity matrix for all 9 MCP tools
noveltyJustification: First lineage pass established the 9-tool registry, dispatch split, and per-tool CLI mapping.

ruledOut.approach: Treat the Python fallback as already covering all advisor tools
ruledOut.reason: The Python CLI only exposes recommend, health, validate, deep-routing, and batch modes; it has no subcommands for rebuild or skill_graph operations.

noveltyJustification: Mapped resident-only services to what a one-shot CLI loses or must emulate.

ruledOut.approach: Daemonless CLI as feature-equivalent without a resident process
ruledOut.reason: The watcher, in-process prompt cache, idle monitor, IPC bridge stats, and generation publishing are resident or daemon-owned capabilities.

focus: KQ3 spec-memory affordance transfer and schema/codegen fit
noveltyJustification: Confirmed the generic CLI pattern transfers, but with skill-advisor-specific schema and trusted-caller deltas.

ruledOut.approach: Hand-author CLI schemas separately from MCP schemas
ruledOut.reason: Advisor Zod schemas are exported and already close to a canonical registry; duplicating them would recreate drift.

focus: KQ4 Python fallback prior art and native-vs-local scorer divergence
noveltyJustification: Central question answered with path coverage, native bridge mechanics, and 10-prompt measured parity.

ruledOut.reason: Measured top-choice parity on 10 prompts and existing batch/fallback affordances make reconciliation lower risk than removal.
ruledOut.evidence: command:10-prompt force-local vs force-native parity measurement, 10/10 same top recommendation

focus: KQ5 long-running operations and build chain
noveltyJustification: Separated read-only validation from write-producing scan/rebuild paths and measured current validation costs.

ruledOut.approach: Run rebuild/scan synchronously on every prompt-submit hook
ruledOut.reason: Rebuild/scan write SQLite and generation state; hook path must remain warm/read-only and fail-open.

noveltyJustification: Counted runtime hook files, bridge/plugin files, MCP registration files, and doctor surfaces.

ruledOut.approach: Assume only Claude/Codex hooks need migration
ruledOut.reason: The runtime surface includes Claude, Gemini, Codex hook, Codex wrapper, Devin, OpenCode plugin, plugin bridge, and MCP configs.

focus: KQ7 hook latency and warm-only policy
noveltyJustification: Measured one-shot and batch costs; established process startup/bridge overhead as the critical hook constraint.

ruledOut.approach: Use one Python-native subprocess per prompt-submit event
ruledOut.reason: Measured median one-shot force-native was 824.8ms, far above the warm-path expectation; batch native median was 277.5ms for 10 prompts, proving startup dominates.

focus: KQ8 coexistence, race classes, and orphan launcher incident
noveltyJustification: Root-caused the orphan class from launcher lifecycle: bridge handoff and detached resident ownership need explicit reap semantics.

ruledOut.approach: Rely only on process exit cleanup
ruledOut.reason: The launcher clears its own lease on normal signal/child exit, but orphan incidents require stale lease and live-process reaping outside the ordinary parent-child path.

focus: KQ9 risk register and implementation design deltas
noveltyJustification: Converted evidence into named implementation deltas with mechanisms and acceptance tests.

ruledOut.approach: Ship a thin recommend-only CLI as the final answer
ruledOut.reason: It would not satisfy zero-feature-loss for rebuild, status, validate, scan/query/status/validate, or trusted-caller propagate semantics.

noveltyJustification: No new source families were needed; this pass reconciled all prior answers into the go/no-go architecture.

ruledOut.approach: Remove the MCP server after CLI introduction
ruledOut.reason: The scope is dual-stack; MCP registration remains the primary rich tool surface while CLI supplies universal resilience.
